name: Academic Merit Scholarship – High School Performance
eligibility: Full-time first-year undergrads who submit their national High School Leaving Certificate (Romanian Bac) and register for 30 ECTS; the one-year tuition percentage is derived from that final grade.
notes: Admissions sets the exact reduction after validating the Bac grade, so confirm with Admissions before quoting Romanian families.

name: Academic Merit Scholarship – CGPA 4.00
eligibility: Continuing students (minimum 30 credits/60 ECTS completed) with a cumulative GPA of 4.00 after the previous academic year and a full 30 ECTS load.
notes: Automatic review each semester from year two onward; use to motivate Romanian students to maintain top grades.

name: Academic Merit Scholarship – CGPA 3.75-3.99
eligibility: Continuing students with CGPA between 3.75 and 3.99 after at least one academic year (30 credits/60 ECTS) on campus.
notes: Applies only from the second year onward and can’t stack with higher-value awards.

name: Academic Merit Scholarship – CGPA 3.50-3.74
eligibility: Continuing students with CGPA between 3.50 and 3.74 after completing a full academic year at UNIC Nicosia.
notes: Flag for Romanian students who narrowly miss the higher tiers so expectations stay realistic.

name: Athletic Scholarship – UNIC Teams
eligibility: Full-time students selected for UNIC athletic teams or clubs; awards are based on sports achievements and potential and may reach 40% of tuition.
notes: Romanian athletes need athletics office clearance and roster commitment; actual percentage depends on performance.

name: Financial Aid Scholarship Committee
eligibility: Students from underprivileged socio-economic backgrounds submit the Financial Aid application; scoring considers family conditions and the economic status of the student’s country of residence.
notes: Romanian households must supply full income documentation; committee points determine the tuition reduction so never quote a percentage before the award letter.

name: Large Family Scholarship
eligibility: Families with four or more children (under 25) can present each child’s birth certificate or a valid big-family card for a 15% tuition reduction across the full programme.
notes: Collect Romanian birth certificates early; this scholarship usually supersedes smaller discounts because UNIC only applies the highest-value award.

name: Academic Family Scholarship
eligibility: UNIC or Intercollege graduates pursuing another degree and siblings enrolled full-time in the same semester each receive a 10% tuition reduction.
notes: Use for Romanian siblings/alumni; note that distance-learning and most postgraduate programmes remain ineligible per policy.

name: Repeated Course Discount
eligibility: Any student repeating a course receives an automatic 50% tuition reduction on that specific module.
notes: Helpful talking point when Romanian students need to resit a unit; highlight that only the highest-value discount per policy applies.

name: Cyprus Government Grant (MOEC)
eligibility: Cypriot citizens or EU citizens who are permanent residents of Cyprus for at least five of the last six years, enrolled on-campus with ≥12 credits and household income ≤€59,000 (+€5,000 per additional dependent).
notes: Romanian students only access this once they or a parent become long-term Cyprus residents; awards range €2,175–€3,420 and unlock extra allowances (€1,800 housing, €1,092 food, €200 travel, €500 first-year).

name: Academic Excellence Scholarship – Platinum
eligibility: Admitted undergrads meeting the Platinum thresholds (Panhellenic ≥19.2/20, IB 44-45, GCE A*AA+, SAT ≥1530, ACT ≥35, or equivalent national exam percentile).
notes: Romanian Bac results fall under the “recognized national examinations may be mapped” clause, so get Admissions to confirm the grade conversion before promising 40%.

name: Academic Excellence Scholarship – Gold
eligibility: Applicants whose final school scores meet the Gold band (Panhellenic 18.5–19.19, IB 40-43, SAT 1450–1520, ACT 33-34, or equivalent).
notes: Ask Admissions to translate Romanian Bac averages into the Gold range; only one Excellence tier applies per student.

name: Academic Excellence Scholarship – Silver
eligibility: Applicants whose final school scores meet the Silver thresholds (Panhellenic 17.5–18.49, IB 37-39, SAT 1350–1440, ACT 31-32, or equivalent).
notes: Romanian averages that map to this band secure 20%; document the conversion in Slate so counselors stay aligned.

name: Academic Excellence Scholarship – Bronze
eligibility: Applicants whose final school scores meet the Bronze tier (Panhellenic 17.0–17.49, IB 34-36, SAT 1250–1340, ACT 29-30, or equivalent).
notes: Use for solid but not top-ranked Romanian students; ensure they know only one Excellence scholarship is granted.

name: Hellenic Frontier Scholarship
eligibility: Full-time undergrads whose permanent address and final two high-school years were in the listed remote Greek islands/border municipalities, with a minimum CPA of 2.50.
notes: Romanian prospects only qualify if they previously lived and studied in those Greek localities; otherwise advise them to pursue other aid.

name: Sports Scholarship – Gold
eligibility: Olympic/Paralympic competitors or medalists at World or European Senior Championships (last 4 years) and equivalent elite performers committed to full-time study.
notes: Romanian Olympians can use national federation letters to prove status; GPA ≥2.50 and anti-doping compliance apply.

name: Sports Scholarship – Silver
eligibility: Current Greek Senior National Team members, top-three finishers at European or World U23/Junior events, national record holders, or top-10 world-ranked esports athletes (or equivalent international status).
notes: UNIC accepts equivalent achievements from other countries, so Romanian federations should certify the athlete’s standing.

name: Sports Scholarship – Bronze
eligibility: Top-three finishers at Panhellenic Senior Championships, Greek U21/U19 national team members, MVPs in U21/U19 national leagues, or equivalent achievements abroad.
notes: Bronze is the entry point for strong Romanian athletes; provide federation records so the Athletics Office can verify equivalency.

name: UNIC Opportunity Scholarship (Need-Based)
eligibility: Full-time residential students from lower or middle-income households; awards are based on a points system that evaluates per-capita income, social factors, health, and residence for EU/EEA applicants equally.
notes: Romanian families must submit the EU-equivalent tax return (e.g., ANAF statement) plus required certificates; reapply annually with updated documents, ideally by 30 June.

name: Large Family Scholarship
eligibility: Families with four or more children who present a family status certificate receive a 15% tuition reduction for the entire programme.
notes: Encourage Romanian households to secure the official certificate before visa filing so the discount can be applied on time.

name: Academic Family Scholarship
eligibility: UNIC or Intercollege graduates pursuing a second degree and siblings enrolled simultaneously at UNIC Athens receive a 10% tuition reduction.
notes: Track Romanian siblings/alumni in Slate to trigger this automatically; not available for distance-learning or medicine programmes.
